response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems experience (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second), but he misses the role's core minimum qualifications. Professional C++ amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — far short of the required 8 years — and his large-scale infrastructure exposure is intermittent rather than sustained. His leadership is informal (workshops, dojos, courses) rather than the required track record of technical leadership mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers on a compute platform, so he is a poor fit for this specific opening despite being a strong engineer overall.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (micro-services at millions of HTTP requests/sec using async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong testing and engineering-practices culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus authored open-source test tooling
Proven knowledge-sharing and mentoring instincts through workshops, multi-day courses, and code dojos
concerns: Fails the hard requirement of 8 years C++ — professional C++ experience is under one year
No formal technical-leadership role owning strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers across teams
Recent experience is product-focused full-stack (TypeScript/Node), not cloud compute platforms or hardware-adjacent infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Limited sustained large-scale infrastructure/cloud platform depth
Distributed systems and mentoring/testing track record
